title: Reinforced Insulated Sheath armor
content:

{{Quote|Let's get started. I'm going to teach you how to construct my masterpiece invention: R.I.S. armor. This armor has no equal in battle. Everything else is junk compared to this!|[[Mol Ni'mai]], to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:P4" />}}
The '''Reinforced Insulated Sheath armor''', or '''R.I.S. armor''' for short, was a form of heavy, yet flexible [[battle armor]] that saw use during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. It could be customized in a variety of [[Color/Legends|colors]]. [[Mol Ni'mai]], a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] master [[armorsmith]] living in the [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], was the inventor of the [[Armor/Legends|armor]]. R.I.S. armor could only be crafted by the most proficient armorsmiths. In addition to its more basic resource requirements, the armor also required the [[Bone/Legends|bones]] and hides of various creatures within [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The armor had eight separate pieces which included two biceps, two bracers, a chest plate, a set of leggings, a helmet, and a pair of [[Boot/Legends|boots]]. During the Galactic Civil War, sometime after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Ni'mai taught a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] and fellow armorsmith how to make the armor. Following this, R.I.S. armor saw use by several disparate organizations throughout the galaxy.

==Description==
{{Quote|They also, however, require some special components. For strength, they require bone fragments from a Woolamander Harrower. For flexibility, they require the hide of a Recluse Gurk King. To provide inner protection and warmth, they require scales from a Giant Dune Kimogila.|Mol Ni'mai, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:P4" />}}
The Reinforced Insulated Sheath was a heavy, yet flexible [[battle armor]]. It provided equal protection to all areas of the body and could be customized in a wide variety of [[Color/Legends|colors]], from [[blue]] to [[black]] to [[red]]. Its protection could be augmented with a [[Personal energy shield/Legends|personal energy shield]]. The [[Armor/Legends|armor's]] construction was particularly difficult, requiring not only the skills of a experienced [[armorsmith]], but many rare materials from across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], in addition to its more mundane material requirements of various [[Mineral/Legends|minerals]] and synthetic materials. The mundane materials were [[Fiberplast/Legends|fiberplast]], [[Copper/Legends|copper]], [[Gemstone/Legends|gemstone]], [[Aluminium/Legends|aluminium]], intrusive and extrusive [[Ore/Legends|ores]], and synthetic cloth. The rare materials were the hide of a [[gurk]] [[King/Legends|king]] and the scales of a giant dune [[kimogila]] from [[Lok/Legends|Lok]], and the [[Bone/Legends|bones]] of a [[Woolamander/Legends|woolamander]] harrower from [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. The armor's appearance layer required the feathers of a [[Peko-peko/Legends|peko-peko]] albatross from [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].<ref name="SWG:P4" />

The armor consisted of eight separate parts that included two bracers, two biceps, a helmet, a chest plate, a set of leggings, and a pair of [[Boot/Legends|boots]]. The helmet included a wide-brimmed top and a visor over the [[Eye/Legends|eye]] holes to protect from [[Star/Legends|sun]] glare. The bracers and biceps had bladed pieces of armor attached. The boots had two spikes protruding from the sides. The leggings were skirt-like, with several pieces of protective armor attached.<ref name="SWG:P4" />

==History==
{{Quote|You believe you have learned all there is to know about being armorsmith. You haven't! I know far more than you ever will, but still there are a few lessons I could teach you. Learning these things will not be easy for someone such as you. It will be expensive in both time and in credits.|Mol Ni'mai, to a spacer|<ref name="SWG:P4" />}}
[[File:MolNimai.png|thumb|right|200px|[[Mol Ni'mai]], the inventor of the R.I.S. armor.]]
The Reinforced Insulated Sheath armor, or R.I.S. armor, was the invention of the [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] master armorsmith [[Mol Ni'mai]]. Sometime after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], at the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Ni'mai was visited by a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]] at [[Mol Ni'mai's hut|his hut]] in the [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]]. The spacer was an armorsmith as well and wished to learn from Ni'mai. Ni'mai demanded 50,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] from the spacer before he would agree to lessons, which the spacer readily paid. Credits in hand, Ni'mai began to instruct the spacer in the construction of R.I.S. armor, explaining the construction of its armor segment and its appearance layer. Once he was satisfied that his student had learned the basics, he tasked the spacer with constructing a pair of R.I.S. boots. The spacer was successful, and Ni'mai rewarded his student with the schematics for the remainder of the armor pieces. The armor became rather prevalent in the galaxy thereafter.<ref name="SWG:P4" />

Individuals to utilize R.I.S. armor included [[Russ Grimblade]] and other members of the [[Hidden Daggers]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] organization, who were known to wear pieces of the armor.<ref name="SWG:JtL">''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed]]''</ref> [[Borantok]] and [[Rhosk]], members of the [[Blackscale Clan/Legends|Blackscale]] [[Slavery/Legends|slaving]] group on the planet [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], also used pieces of the armor, as did several [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Chiss Poachers|poachers]] within the [[Rodian Hunting Grounds|Etyyy]] region of Kashyyyk.<ref name="SWG:RotW">''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Rage of the Wookiees]]''</ref> A group of bandits known as the [[Razor Runners]] on the planet [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]] employed the armor heavily amongst its members.<ref name="SWG:ToOW">''[[Star Wars Galaxies: Trials of Obi-Wan]]''</ref><ref name="SWGTCG">{{SWGTCG|set=Threat of the Conqueror|cardname=Razor Runner Commando}}</ref> The armor was also used by the [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] of the [[Galactic Vacant Building Demolitions]] company.<ref name="SWG:QZVCD">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=event|House Demolition}}</ref>

R.I.S. armors were often used by elite soldiers of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], including [[Rebel dark trooper hunter]]s, [[Weapons master/Legends|weapons masters]], [[Sith Hunter]]s,<ref name="SWG:P6">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=other|Publish 6: The Imperial Crackdown}}</ref> and sentries.<ref name="SWG:TtR">{{SWGcite|exp=NGE|type=faction|Imperial|Thwarting the Rebels|Talus}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Reinforced Insulated Sheath armor was a type of heavy armor in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:P4" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> The armor, along with its designer and the quest to learn how to craft it, were added to the game with the "[[W:c:swg:Publish 4 Notes: 13 November 03|Publish 4]]," released on [[November 13]], 2003.<ref name="SonyCite-Publish 4 Mounts and Cities">{{SonyCite|url=recent_updates.jsp?o=60891|text=Publish 4 Mounts and Cities}}</ref>

R.I.S. armor is of the "Battle" type within the game, meaning it provides equal protection from energy and kinetic damage sources. The player had to be a master armorsmith to craft the armor.<ref name="SWG:P4" />

Prior to the addition of [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]] to the game,<ref name="SonyCite-Publish 8: Droids Rebuilt">{{SonyCite|url=recent_updates.jsp?o=61943|text=Publish 8: Droids Rebuilt}}</ref> the R.I.S. armor was the most difficult to construct, as it required many rare items from creatures both difficult to find and to defeat.<ref name="SWG:P4" /> Prior to [[2005]] and the release of the "[[W:c:swg:New Game Enhancements|New Game Enhancements]]" for ''Star Wars Galaxies'',<ref name="SWG:SK">{{GalaxiesNGE}}</ref> the peko-peko feathers gave a health bonus when used to construct the armor. Additionally, the [[Acklay/Legends|acklay]] within the [[Geonosian Bio-Lab]] on Yavin 4 could drop a schematic for an Interwoven R.I.S. Armor Segment.<ref name="SWG:P4" /> However, with the release of the ''NGE'', this item no longer drops, and if a character has already learned the schematic, crafting it produces a normal R.I.S. armor segment.<ref name="SWG:SK" /> Like the majority of armor in the game, R.I.S. armor can be acquired for free while playing on the game's test center.<ref name="SWG:P4" />
